preamble: The appearance of a velar nasal [ŋ] where no consonant originally existed (epenthesis) is relatively uncommon but can occur through nasalization assimilation. When a vowel is nasalized before a subsequent nasal consonant, the nasal quality may be 'reinterpreted' as an actual consonant in rapid speech or in certain morphological constructions.
phoneticEffects: Epenthesis, Nasal Assimilation, Consonantization of Nasalized Vowel

examples.note: Assimilation of /n/ to the following velar stop [k] in 'five' may create a temporary [ŋ] even in hypercorrection or casual speech when the stop is dropped.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
